topic: Cloud Dataflow and BigQuery ETL pipeline
question_text: How would you design a highly scalable, fault-tolerant ETL pipeline using Cloud Dataflow and BigQuery for real-time analytics, handling schema evolution and data quality?
hook_text: Build a real-time ETL pipeline on Google Cloud that scales automatically and ensures data quality. Here’s how!
cta_text: Master Dataflow and BigQuery for your next cloud project. Follow for more deep dives!

answer_sections.title: Ingestion and Staging with Pub/Sub
answer_sections.text: Ingest real-time data via Pub/Sub. Land raw data in Cloud Storage. Utilize Dataflow Flex Templates for staging. This ensures resilient, scalable input.
answer_sections.spoken_audio: Pub/Sub ingests real-time data, landing it in Cloud Storage. Dataflow Flex Templates handle robust staging.

answer_sections.title: Dataflow Transformations & Quality
answer_sections.text: Dataflow performs complex transformations, aggregations, and data quality checks using Apache Beam. Implement UDFs for custom logic, DLT for dead-letter. Manage schema.
answer_sections.spoken_audio: Dataflow executes complex transformations, aggregations, and quality checks with Apache Beam and UDFs.

answer_sections.title: BigQuery Loading & Monitoring
answer_sections.text: Load transformed data into BigQuery. Leverage streaming inserts for real-time. Use partitioning and clustering for performance. Monitor pipeline via Stackdriver.
answer_sections.spoken_audio: Load transformed data into BigQuery using streaming inserts. Monitor pipeline health and performance with Stackdriver.

diagrams.title: Data Transformation
diagrams.dsl: [[Cloud Storage]] -> [Dataflow (Apache Beam)] -> {Data Quality & Schema Checks} -> [[Staging BigQuery]]

diagrams.title: Loading & Monitoring
diagrams.dsl: [[Staging BigQuery]] -> [BigQuery Streaming Inserts] -> [[Final BigQuery Table]] -> (Stackdriver Monitoring)
